title: Cold vs influenza vs COVID-19 vs strep throat vs allergies
summary: Symptoms overlap heavily, so a table can guide suspicion but cannot make a diagnosis. Onset pattern, fever, itch, exposure, testing and examination provide the most useful distinctions.
evidenceSnapshot: Influenza often starts abruptly with prominent systemic symptoms; allergies commonly cause itch and repeated sneezing without fever; strep classically causes sore throat and fever without cough. COVID-19 can resemble either a cold or flu and may require testing.

interventions.name: Use pattern recognition cautiously
interventions.finding: Combinations of symptoms shift probability but are not definitive.
interventions.practical: Compare onset, fever, cough, nasal itch, aches and exposures.
interventions.mechanism: Different diseases have typical but overlapping inflammatory patterns.
interventions.cautions: Young children, older adults and immunocompromised people may present atypically.

interventions.name: Targeted testing
interventions.finding: Can identify COVID-19, influenza or group A streptococcus when the result would change treatment or precautions.
interventions.practical: Follow current Australian testing advice and clinician guidance.
interventions.mechanism: Detects pathogen antigen, nucleic acid or immune response.
interventions.cautions: A negative test can be falsely reassuring if timing or sampling is poor.

interventions.name: Clinical examination
interventions.finding: Assesses tonsils, lymph nodes, breathing, hydration, ears and complications.
interventions.practical: Seek review for severe sore throat, breathing difficulty, persistent fever or high-risk status.
interventions.mechanism: Adds objective findings and differential diagnosis.
interventions.cautions: Online symptom comparison cannot detect every complication.

practicalSteps: Treat the comparison table as a probability guide, not a verdict.
Test when a result changes isolation, antivirals, antibiotics or contact with vulnerable people.
Reassess if the illness changes course or lasts longer than expected.
Antibiotics are for confirmed or strongly suspected bacterial disease, not routine colds, influenza or COVID-19.
avoidOrCare: Do not diagnose strep only from white spots; viral tonsillitis can look similar.
Do not rule out COVID-19 because symptoms are mild or include a runny nose.
Do not assume loss of smell is unique to COVID-19; severe congestion and other causes can contribute.

myths.reality: They are more typical and often stronger in influenza, but COVID-19 and other infections can also cause them.
Absence of cough is only one feature; testing or clinical scoring is needed when strep is suspected.

redFlags: Drooling, inability to swallow saliva, muffled voice, neck swelling or breathing difficulty
Severe one-sided throat pain, trismus or rapidly worsening swelling
Breathlessness, chest pain, confusion, dehydration or blue/grey colour
High-risk person with possible influenza or COVID-19 who may qualify for early treatment
unsettled: Symptom profiles change with variants, vaccination, prior immunity, age and co-infection.

comparisonRows.COVID-19: Test/precautions and early treatment assessment if eligible
comparisonRows.Strep throat: Clinical assessment and test when indicated
